pr.title: ft/ftKirby: decompile 7 Kirby special N functions
pr.author: johnwinston

summary: Merged a Kirby special-N decompilation slice replacing stubs in ftKb_Init, ftKb_SpecialN, ftKb_SpecialNNs, and ftKb_SpecialNZd. The diff implements inhale drink/swallow animation callbacks, copied Sheik/Mewtwo/Jigglypuff neutral-B behavior, and Kirby hat material-animation loading. The title/body say "7 functions", but the diff names eight public stub replacements, so the exact count is slightly inconsistent in the available evidence.

key_files.role: Decompiled ftKb_SpecialNDrink0_Anim, ftKb_SpecialNDrink1_Anim, and ftKb_SpecialAirNDrink_Anim; added ftCo_Throw, ftCo_ThrownKirby, and ftanim includes.
Decompiled copied Sheik Needle Storm loop IASA callbacks and copied Mewtwo Shadow Ball start entry; added fn_80106DB0 forward declaration.
Decompiled ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C, the Kirby hat material-animation attachment path; added ftanim include.
Decompiled ftKb_PrSpecialAirNStart_Anim for copied Jigglypuff/Purin aerial Rollout startup.
Updated ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C prototype from UNK_RET to void.

what_changed: Replaced the ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C stub with code that indexes ftKb_Init_803C9FC8 by copied ability/costume, walks HSD_MatAnimJoint data, skips parts via ftParts_8007506C, and attaches matanims to Kirby hat DObjs with HSD_DObjAddAnimAll.

Implemented ftKb_SpecialNDrink0_Anim and ftKb_SpecialNDrink1_Anim as one-shot swallow/drink animation callbacks gated by cmd_vars[0] and target_item_gobj; they call ftCommon_8007E2F4, play SFX 0x222F6, notify the item with it_802F28C8, clear x1A64/target_item_gobj/cmd_vars[0], then transition on animation end.
Implemented ftKb_SpecialAirNDrink_Anim for aerial swallow handling using victim_gobj, ftCo_800DE2CC, ftCo_800BE000, ftCo_800BD9E0, and ftKb_SpecialN_800F1BAC before falling on animation completion.

Implemented ftKb_SkSpecialNLoop_IASA and ftKb_SkSpecialAirNLoop_IASA: releasing B changes to the appropriate end motion state, clears mv.kb.specialhi.x0, runs ftKirbyDmgInline, and sets accessory4_cb to fn_80106DB0; pressing LR changes to the appropriate cancel state.

Implemented ftKb_SpecialNMt_80107568 as a thin wrapper around a new static inline ftKb_SpecialNMt_ChangeAction, which enters ftKb_MS_MtSpecialNStart, clears cmd_vars, zeroes vertical velocity and copied-special state fields, installs damage/death callbacks, conditionally loads specialn_mt_frames_to_transition, and calls ftAnim_8006EBA4.

Implemented ftKb_PrSpecialAirNStart_Anim: after startup animation finishes it changes to ftKb_MS_PrSpecialAirNFull, installs callbacks, freezes animation rate, sets tiny horizontal self velocity, clears anim velocity, and rotates the model part with ftPartSetRotY.
Synchronized headers/includes with the new code by adding needed ftanim/throw headers and changing ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C from an unknown return type to void.
smart_moves: Grouped related Kirby neutral-B/copy-ability stubs in one PR, making repeated state-machine patterns visible across ground, air, copied Sheik, copied Mewtwo, and copied Jigglypuff code.
Kept one-shot side effects behind cmd_vars[0] plus non-null target/victim checks, then cleared the triggering fields so animation callbacks do not repeat object-transfer logic every frame.
Used a local anonymous struct overlay for the hat costume data table rather than inventing a broad public struct name without evidence.

Separated Mewtwo Shadow Ball entry logic into a static inline helper while leaving the exported address-named function as a small wrapper, which is a useful pattern when matching entry thunks or stack layout.
Updated the header return type to void once the implementation proved there was no return value, reducing future UNK_RET leakage.
decomp_lessons: For Kirby special/copy routines, do not assume the existing field names describe the current move: this PR uses mv.kb.specialhi fields inside copied Sheik and Mewtwo neutral-B logic.
Animation callbacks often perform gameplay side effects first, gated by command variables, and only then check ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining for motion-state transitions.
Ground/air variants may be structurally identical but require exact motion-state constants, literal forms, callback assignments, and stack padding to match.

When decompiling asset-loading paths, a small local data-table overlay plus existing traversal helpers such as ftAnim_GetNextMatAnimJointInTree can be enough evidence; avoid over-generalizing table structs prematurely.

Function-count claims in autogenerated PR text can drift: here the title/body says 7 functions while the diff visibly replaces eight public stub markers, so future searches should key off symbols as well as the PR title.
Keep includes and prototypes in sync with decompiled call sites; header cleanup is part of a matching PR, not a separate afterthought.
naming_conventions: Kirby fighter functions use the ftKb_ prefix.
Copied-character neutral-B names embed character abbreviations after SpecialN, such as Sk for Sheik, Mt for Mewtwo, and Pr for Purin/Jigglypuff.
Callback suffixes follow the engine role: _Anim for animation callbacks and _IASA for input/interrupt callbacks.
State-phase names use Start, Loop, End, Cancel, Full, Drink, and Air to distinguish motion-state variants.
Address-suffixed names such as ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C and ftKb_SpecialNMt_80107568 remain when the PR does not establish a stronger semantic name.
Headers retain address comments like /* 0EF35C */ alongside function prototypes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(8) in ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C, ftKb_PrSpecialAirNStart_Anim, and ftKb_SpecialNMt_80107568, and PAD_STACK(32) in ftKb_SpecialAirNDrink_Anim.
Used the comma-expression form Fighter* fp = (0, GET_FIGHTER(gobj)); in the two ground Drink callbacks, indicating codegen-sensitive matching.
Declared a static const motion flag mask in ftKb_PrSpecialAirNStart_Anim as (1 << 1) | (1 << 4) | (1 << 18).
Preserved a nested block that redeclares Fighter* fp after Fighter_ChangeMotionState in ftKb_PrSpecialAirNStart_Anim, likely to match register/lifetime behavior.
Zeroed cmd_vars in descending order in ftKb_SpecialNMt_ChangeAction and used a conditional timer initialized to 0 before loading dat_attrs->specialn_mt_frames_to_transition.
Matched Sheik ground/air IASA variants with nearly identical control flow but exact ground/air motion-state constants and call arguments.
review_feedback: No human review comments or formal reviews are present in the slice.
decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code at 59.39%, +0.01% and +440 bytes.
The bot listed ftKb_SkSpecialNLoop_IASA and ftKb_SkSpecialAirNLoop_IASA as new 100% matches.

The same bot report listed several initial partial improvements: ftKb_PrSpecialAirNStart_Anim 99.98%, ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C 96.64%, ftKb_SpecialNMt_80107568 98.56%, ftKb_SpecialAirNDrink_Anim 95.20%, and Drink0/Drink1 99.80%; the PR body separately claims all 7 functions were later verified 100% by an overnight script.
Evidence for reviewer intent is weak because the only recorded discussion is the automated decomp.dev report.
